source_files.headline: Jalen Brunson scores 33 points and helps Knicks take 3-0 lead over 76ers with 108-94 Game 3 win

Joel Embiid.injury_context: Returned from ankle/hip issues; mobility and conditioning still capped impact.

Tyrese Maxey.usage_note: Efficient but not enough volume to offset Knicks possession edge.

game_phase_analysis.Q1: Philadelphia opened hot behind Paul George's 15-point burst and led by as many as 12, but New York stayed efficient and preserved the offensive glass.
game_phase_analysis.Q2: New York flipped the game 33-21. Bridges, Brunson, Shamet, and the bench stabilized the offense while Philadelphia's shot quality and ball security degraded.
game_phase_analysis.Q3: Philadelphia cut the deficit to two late in the third, but New York answered through Brunson free throws and Shamet's late three to restore a nine-point cushion.
game_phase_analysis.Q4: Knicks closed with composure. Brunson, Bridges, and Hart generated the decisive separation, while Philadelphia scored only 18 points in the period.

pivotal_moments.event: Jose Alvarado transition three gave NYK first lead at 38-35
Mikal Bridges 25-foot three pushed NYK to 52-42
Landry Shamet three stretched NYK lead to 85-76
Brunson three made it 95-86 after PHI trimmed to four
Bridges fast-break score pushed lead to 105-89

model_relevance_summary.liveflow_read: Correct. Halftime NYK -5.5 captured the moment when possession control, rebounding, and Philly Q1 heater regression converged.
rolling_window_translation: Knicks remain highly live in road playoff environments when Brunson controls Q2/Q4 usage.
Philadelphia early hot starts should not be over-weighted when driven by a single scorer heater.
Without OG Anunoby, Knicks can still stabilize via Hart/Bridges/Shamet minutes, but rotation fatigue should be watched if series extends.
Embiid active status alone is not enough to upgrade PHI if mobility, rebounding, and transition defense remain compromised.
NYK second-chance and defensive rebounding advantage is now a high-weight series signal.

rolling_window_weight.reason: Competitive through three quarters before Knicks' closing execution separated; more predictive than Game 1 blowout and validates Game 2 close-game profile.
